name: DOGE Range Failure Reacceptance v1
thesis: DOGE 4h is the best-fit primary lane for a chop-first range-failure reacceptance test. The edge should come from broad reacceptance after a false break, not exact one-bar reversal timing.
edge_mechanism: range failure mean reversion after value reacceptance
invalidation_condition: Abort if testing shows weak blind-window participation, contradictory behavior between excursion and reacceptance, or no post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: False breaks from local balance that reverse once price reaccepts value in a predominantly choppy regime.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: In choppy DOGE 4h conditions, directional probes often fail. Once price reclaims the value area, trapped extension traders can fuel a move back toward balance.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h is the correct primary lane because the live regime is 90% CHOP and DOGE tends to overshoot local balance before snapping back.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If the move keeps trending after the excursion instead of reaccepting value, mean-reversion entries will be too early.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but conversions are weak, tighten the take-profit target before making the trigger more selective.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; excursion and reacceptance roles complement each other
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by proximity-to-band logic instead of exact reversal-candle requirements

long: Take profit: EMA_50 or 1.2 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
short: Take profit: EMA_50 or 1.2 * ATR_14, whichever comes first
